description: Real measured scaling curve of the distributed ML-DSA-65 aggregation (mama-party.x, malicious dishonest-majority, security_parameter=40). One MPC run per N (kappa=0 accepted on the first attempt). Numbers are from real ps sampling and real manifest/party-log parsing; failures are real.
honesty_boundary: small-scale real distributed masks over a trusted-setup dealt-then-shared threshold key; NOT dealerless, NOT no-single-secret, NOT production, NOT the 6,667-signer scale. Extrapolations are power-law fits over the measured points and are labelled as extrapolation, not measurement.

rows.failure_reason: MPC run not clean at N=6: all-pairs OT traffic exhausted OS network buffers (ENOBUFS 'No buffer space available' -> OT-thread fatal buffer desync) under memory pressure (swap peaked 10.7 GB); exit codes [-13,1,1,1,1,1], no outputs, no completion. Real saturation, not a clean abort.
rows.failure_root_cause: macOS kernel socket-buffer exhaustion (ENOBUFS): every OT thread died with 'network receive error: No buffer space available' / 'Bad receive buffer size' (desynced length prefixes up to ~4.16 GB vs 4 MB buffers); party 0 then took SIGPIPE (-13) writing to the dead peers. all_outputs_present=False, completion marker absent -> no signature produced.

reference_n3.note: prior reference run (not part of this sweep)
